items.question: She says she ___ tired. (present reporting verb)
explanations: Correct! After a present reporting verb ('says'), keep the present: 'is tired'.

items.question: The teacher told us that water ___ at 100°C. (general truth)
explanations: Correct! A general truth can stay in the present: 'water boils'.
'Boiled' is possible but the present highlights the timeless fact.

items.question: He said he ___ help us move on Saturday. (modal)
explanations: Correct! 'Will' backshifts to 'would', which then doesn't change further.
'Would have' changes the meaning to the past.

items.question: She told me she ___ in Rome. (still true now)
explanations: Correct! Still true, so you can keep the present: 'she lives in Rome'.
'Lived' is also possible, but 'lives' shows it's still true.
'Had lived' suggests she no longer does.

items.question: They said we ___ leave before dark. (modal)
explanations: Correct! 'Must' doesn't change in reported speech.

items.question: My friend says the shop ___ at nine. (present reporting verb)
explanations: Correct! With 'says', keep the present: 'opens at nine'.

items.question: She said she ___ swim when she was young. (modal, unchanged)
explanations: Correct! 'Can' backshifts to 'could', which then stays.

items.question: Correct this: 'She says she was hungry.'
options: She says she had been hungry.
She says she would be hungry.
explanations: Correct! A present reporting verb keeps the present: 'is hungry'.
'Says … was' mismatches a present reporting verb.

items.question: Correct this: 'He said he would helped us.'
options: He said he would help us.
He said he would helped us.
He said he will help us.
He said he helped would us.

items.question: When is backshift NOT required?
options: With a present reporting verb or a still-true fact.
explanations: Correct! Present reporting verbs and still-true/general facts don't require backshift.
Backshift is common but not always required.
